response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates solid software engineering fundamentals and communication skills, this profile is a poor fit for the role. The position mandates 5-10 years of professional C++ development on Windows platforms; the resume shows approximately 6 months of professional C++ experience at National Instruments plus academic projects. More critically, the role requires domain expertise in capital markets, investment banking, or fixed-income systems—the candidate's career has focused entirely on web, mobile, GIS, and media platforms with zero exposure to financial services. The combination of insufficient C++ depth and complete absence of capital markets domain experience creates a fundamental mismatch.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD infrastructure, code quality focus) applicable to critical systems
Proven ability to communicate complex technical concepts through workshops, talks, and open-source documentation
Full-stack technical versatility shows adaptability across multiple languages and platforms
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience versus the required 5-10 years; most recent work is TypeScript/Node.js
Zero experience in capital markets, investment banking, fixed-income, or derivatives domains—all stated as required expertise
No demonstrated Windows platform or SQL Server/Oracle database expertise; recent career focused on web/open-source rather than financial systems

key_factors.factor: C++ depth gap (6 months vs 5-10 years required)
Windows/SQL Server platform specificity not demonstrated
